Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotBorder

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotBorder
public class PotBorder extends Object
Represents a border style for shapes, including color, width, dash pattern, and line caps.

This class is immutable; all modification methods return a new instance. It provides factory methods for common border styles and constants for predefined borders. The border width is specified in points, and opacity is a value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque). A border with a width of zero or less is considered "none" and will not be rendered.

Usage Example


 // Create a solid black border
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0, PotColor.BLACK);

 // Create a dashed red border
 PotBorder dashedRed = PotBorder.dashed(1.5, PotColor.RED);

 // Modify an existing border
 PotBorder modified = border.withColor(PotColor.BLUE).withWidth(3.0);

 // Use a predefined border
 PotBorder thinGray = PotBorder.GRAY_THIN;
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    PotBorder.CapStyle
    Defines the shape of the ends of a border line.
    static enum 
    PotBorder.DashStyle
    Defines the dash pattern for a border line.
    static enum 
    PotBorder.JoinStyle
    Defines the shape of the junction where two border lines meet.

  • Field Details

    • NONE

      public static final PotBorder NONE
      A border with zero width, representing no visible border.

    • BLACK_THIN

      public static final PotBorder BLACK_THIN
      A solid black border with a width of 1 point.

    • BLACK_MEDIUM

      public static final PotBorder BLACK_MEDIUM
      A solid black border with a width of 2 points.

    • BLACK_THICK

      public static final PotBorder BLACK_THICK
      A solid black border with a width of 3 points.

    • GRAY_THIN

      public static final PotBorder GRAY_THIN
      A solid gray border with a width of 1 point.

  • Method Details

    • solid

      public static PotBorder solid(double width, PotColor color)
      Creates a solid border with the specified width and color.

      The border will have solid dash style, flat cap style, round join style, and full opacity.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.5, PotColor.RED);
      Parameters:
      width - the border width in points.
      color - the border color.
      Returns:
      a new solid border instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if color is null.

    • solid

      public static PotBorder solid(double width)
      Creates a solid black border with the specified width.

      The border will have solid dash style, flat cap style, round join style, and full opacity.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(1.0);
      Parameters:
      width - the border width in points.
      Returns:
      a new solid black border instance.

    • solid

      public static PotBorder solid()
      Creates a solid black border with a default width of 1 point.

      This is a convenience method for the most common border style.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder defaultBorder = PotBorder.solid();
      Returns:
      a solid black border with a width of 1 point.
      Since:
      1.0

    • dashed

      public static PotBorder dashed(double width, PotColor color)
      Creates a dashed border with the specified width and color.

      The border will have dash dash style, flat cap style, round join style, and full opacity.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.dashed(1.0, PotColor.GREEN);
      Parameters:
      width - the border width in points.
      color - the border color.
      Returns:
      a new dashed border instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if color is null.

    • dotted

      public static PotBorder dotted(double width, PotColor color)
      Creates a dotted border with the specified width and color.

      The border will have dot dash style, round cap style, round join style, and full opacity. Round caps are used to ensure dots appear circular.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.dotted(0.5, PotColor.BLUE);
      Parameters:
      width - the border width in points.
      color - the border color.
      Returns:
      a new dotted border instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if color is null.

    • dashDot

      public static PotBorder dashDot(double width, PotColor color)
      Creates a dash-dot border with the specified width and color.

      The border will have dash-dot dash style, flat cap style, round join style, and full opacity.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.dashDot(1.5, PotColor.ORANGE);
      Parameters:
      width - the border width in points.
      color - the border color.
      Returns:
      a new dash-dot border instance.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if color is null.

    • none

      public static PotBorder none()
      Returns a border instance representing no visible border.

      This is equivalent to the NONE constant. The border has zero width, so it will not be rendered.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder invisible = PotBorder.none();
      Returns:
      the PotBorder.NONE instance.

    • withColor

      public PotBorder withColor(PotColor color)
      Creates a new border with the specified color.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its color. The original instance remains unchanged.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0, PotColor.RED);
 PotBorder blueBorder = border.withColor(PotColor.BLUE);
      Parameters:
      color - the new color for the border; if null, PotColor.BLACK is used.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified color.
      See Also:
      • color(PotColor)

    • color

      public PotBorder color(PotColor color)
      Creates a new border with the specified color.

      This is a fluent alias for withColor(PotColor). Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its color. The original instance remains unchanged.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0).color(PotColor.GREEN);
      Parameters:
      color - the new color for the border; if null, PotColor.BLACK is used.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified color.
      See Also:
      • withColor(PotColor)

    • withWidth

      public PotBorder withWidth(double width)
      Creates a new border with the specified width.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its width. The original instance remains unchanged. The width is clamped to be non-negative.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(1.0);
 PotBorder thickBorder = border.withWidth(3.0);
      Parameters:
      width - the new width for the border in points; negative values are treated as zero.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified width.
      See Also:
      • width(double)

    • width

      public PotBorder width(double width)
      Creates a new border with the specified width.

      This is a fluent alias for withWidth(double). Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its width. The original instance remains unchanged. The width is clamped to be non-negative.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.dashed(1.0).width(5.0);
      Parameters:
      width - the new width for the border in points; negative values are treated as zero.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified width.
      See Also:
      • withWidth(double)

    • withDashStyle

      public PotBorder withDashStyle(PotBorder.DashStyle dashStyle)
      Creates a new border with the specified dash style.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its dash style. The original instance remains unchanged.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0);
 PotBorder dashedBorder = border.withDashStyle(DashStyle.DASH);
      Parameters:
      dashStyle - the new dash style for the border; if null, PotBorder.DashStyle.SOLID is used.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified dash style.

    • withCapStyle

      public PotBorder withCapStyle(PotBorder.CapStyle capStyle)
      Creates a new border with the specified line cap style.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its line cap style. The original instance remains unchanged.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.dotted(1.0);
 PotBorder roundedBorder = border.withCapStyle(CapStyle.ROUND);
      Parameters:
      capStyle - the new line cap style for the border; if null, PotBorder.CapStyle.FLAT is used.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified cap style.

    • withJoinStyle

      public PotBorder withJoinStyle(PotBorder.JoinStyle joinStyle)
      Creates a new border with the specified line join style.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its line join style. The original instance remains unchanged.

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0);
 PotBorder beveledBorder = border.withJoinStyle(JoinStyle.BEVEL);
      Parameters:
      joinStyle - the new line join style for the border; if null, PotBorder.JoinStyle.ROUND is used.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified join style.

    • withOpacity

      public PotBorder withOpacity(double opacity)
      Creates a new border with the specified opacity.

      Returns a new PotBorder instance that is identical to this one except for its opacity. The original instance remains unchanged. The opacity value is clamped to the range [0.0, 1.0].

      Usage Example

      
 PotBorder border = PotBorder.solid(2.0);
 PotBorder semiTransparentBorder = border.withOpacity(0.5);
      Parameters:
      opacity - the new opacity for the border, where 0.0 is fully transparent and 1.0 is fully opaque.
      Returns:
      a new PotBorder with the specified opacity.

    • getColor

      public PotColor getColor()
      Returns the color of this border.
      Returns:
      the border color.

    • getWidth

      public double getWidth()
      Returns the width of this border.
      Returns:
      the border width in points.

    • getDashStyle

      public PotBorder.DashStyle getDashStyle()
      Returns the dash style of this border.
      Returns:
      the border dash style.

    • getCapStyle

      public PotBorder.CapStyle getCapStyle()
      Returns the line cap style of this border.
      Returns:
      the border line cap style.

    • getJoinStyle

      public PotBorder.JoinStyle getJoinStyle()
      Returns the line join style of this border.
      Returns:
      the border line join style.

    • getOpacity

      public double getOpacity()
      Returns the opacity of this border.
      Returns:
      the border opacity in the range [0.0, 1.0].

    • isNone

      public boolean isNone()
      Determines whether this border is effectively invisible.

      A border is considered "none" if its width is less than or equal to zero.

      Returns:
      true if the border width is zero or negative, false otherwise.

    • getWidthEmu

      public long getWidthEmu()
      Returns the width of this border in English Metric Units (EMU).

      This is a convenience method for converting the point-based width to the EMU unit commonly used in presentation file formats like PPTX.

      Returns:
      the border width in EMU.
      See Also:
      • UnitConverter.pointsToEmu(double)

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
